mapping.a_element: closed-form one-step analytical solution / analysis of prediction MSE
mapping.b_element: non-asymptotic prediction error bound / approximation theory

brief_justification: Both do time-series forecasting and carry some theoretical error analysis, but the machinery differs decisively: paper A is a minimal, nearly-nonparametric in-context mechanism that forecasts by linearly blending the current latent state with the nearest in-context neighbor and its successor (zero-shot, two parameters, context-parroting limit), whereas paper B is a trained depth-separable deep neural network with cross-stage parameter sharing that nonlinearly aligns data of different sampling frequencies. Nearest-neighbor in-context blending and depth-separable frequency-alignment networks are unrelated processes, so no role-for-role mapping of the driving mechanism holds.
